node_kinds.name: jamf_Account
node_kinds.description: A JAMF account that authenticates to the tenant

node_kinds.name: jamf_Computer
node_kinds.description: A macOS or other computer enrolled to JAMF

node_kinds.name: jamf_Site
node_kinds.description: A site in the JAMF tenant

node_kinds.name: jamf_ApiClient
node_kinds.description: An API client created in the JAMF tenant that can be assigned roles

node_kinds.name: jamf_DisabledApiClient
node_kinds.description: An API client in the JAMF tenant that is disabled

node_kinds.name: jamf_ComputerUser
node_kinds.description: An end-user assigned to a computer device in a JAMF tenant

node_kinds.name: jamf_DisabledAccount
node_kinds.description: A JAMF Pro account that is not active

node_kinds.name: jamf_SSOIntegration
node_kinds.description: An IDP registered with the JAMF tenant for authentication using SSO

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Contains
relationship_kinds.description: The source contains the target resource.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_AdminTo
relationship_kinds.description: The source has full administrative control over the target and all resources controlled by the target.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_AdminToSite
relationship_kinds.description: The source has administrative control over the site and all resources controlled by the site. This includes creating policies that impact resources of the site, send or clear MDM commands, remotely administer site devices and computers,  create computer objects for the site.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_UpdateAccounts
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Update Accounts' JSS Object permission which allows altering the permissions of existing accounts or groups. If the source is a local Jamf account they can grant themself additional permissions, grant permissions to other accounts, grant permissions to any existing groups, modify members of groups to include adding themselves if the source is a Jamf account, enable disabled accounts, and reset the password of any Jamf account.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_CreateAccounts
relationship_kinds.description: he account possesses the 'Create Accounts' JSS Object permission which allows creating new accounts, including administrators, as well as creating new groups with any permissions and defining Jamf accounts assigned to them.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_ScriptsNonTraversable
relationship_kinds.description: The source can create or update scripts on the target.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_CreatePolicies
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Create Policies' privilege allowing code execution on the target.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_UpdatePolicies
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Update Policies' privilege and at least one policy already exists in the tenant allowing code execution on the target.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_MemberOf
relationship_kinds.description: The source node is a member of the destination node.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_CreateComputerExtensions
relationship_kinds.description: The source can create computer extension attributes executing code on all computers in the tenant.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_UpdateComputerExtensions
relationship_kinds.description: The source can update computer extension attributes and at least one computer extension attribute exists allowing execution of code on all computers in the tenant.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Create_API_Client_and_Create_Role
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Create API Integrations' and 'Create API Roles' permissions, which together allow the creation of new API clients with any permissions in newly assigned roles and retrieving credentials to authenticate with the permissions of the new client.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Create_API_Client_and_Update_Role
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Create API Integrations' and 'Update API Roles' permissions and at least one API role exists in the tenant. Combined these allow the creation of new API clients to assume roles, modifying the permissions of existing roles, and retrieving credentials to authenticate with the permissions of the new client.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Create_API_Client_and_Assign_Role
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Create API Integrations' permission and at least one role has been created in the tenant. Combined these allow the creation of new API clients to assume the permissions of existing roles and retrieving credentials to authenticate with the permissions of the new client.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Update_API_Client_and_Update_Roles
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Update API Integrations' and 'Update API Roles' permissions and at least one Api Client and Role exist in the tenant, which together allow upedating existing API clients with any permissions by updating existing roles.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Update_API_Client_and_Create_Roles
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Update API Integrations' and 'Create API Roles' permissions and at least one API client exists in the tenant. Combined these allow updating API clients and assigning new roles created with any included permissions.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Update_API_Client_and_Assign_Role
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Update API Integrations' permission and at least one role has been created in the tenant. Combined these allow updating existing API clients to assume the permissions of existing roles.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Update_Self_and_Update_Roles
relationship_kinds.description: The source is an API Client that possesses 'Update API Integrations' and 'Update API Roles' permissions and at least one Role exists in the tenant, which together allows the client to update itself or other Api clients to assign any permissions by updating and assigning a role.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Update_Self_and_Create_Roles
relationship_kinds.description: The source is an API client that possesses the 'Update API Integrations' and 'Create API Roles' permissions. Combined these allow the API client to update itself or other Api clients and assigning new roles created with any included permissions.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Update_Self_and_Assign_Roles
relationship_kinds.description: The source is an API client that possesses the 'Update API Integrations' permission and at least one role has been created in the tenant. Combined these allow the Api client to update itself to assume the permissions of existing roles.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_CreateAPIRoles
relationship_kinds.description: The source can create API Roles in the JAMF tenant.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_UpdateAPIRoles
relationship_kinds.description: The source can update API Roles in the Jamf tenant.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_AssignedUser
relationship_kinds.description: The specified user is assigned to the source computer.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_MatchedEmail
relationship_kinds.description: The Jamf principal email attribute matched the Jamf account email indicating it is likely the same account.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_MatchedName
relationship_kinds.description: The Jamf principal name or displayname attributes matched the Jamf account name.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Update_Recurring_Scripts
relationship_kinds.description: The source possesses the 'Update Scripts' permission and there are scripts configured to run repeatedly on target computers in the Jamf tenant. This allows the source to update specific scripts to execute code on the in-scope computers for the policy.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_SSO_Login
relationship_kinds.description: SSO Sources can map attributes to authenticate and inherit the privileges of the target.

relationship_kinds.name: jamf_Okta_Same_Device
relationship_kinds.description: The Jamf Pro registered computer UDID matches the registered device UDID value in Okta.
